title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for multipart upload state, parts, checksums, and cleanup and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for recoverable multipart-upload state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
Persist upload ID, part number, offset, size, checksum, ETag, attempt, and owner so completion uses the exact accepted part list.
Bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent goroutine or buffer leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Abort Lifecycle Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Abort Lifecycle Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 8137 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Abort Lifecycle Cleanup.
steps.why: Retrieving Abort Lifecycle Cleanup through the concrete recoverable multipart-upload state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Abort Lifecycle Cleanup, keep Abort Lifecycle Cleanup connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.
